Delegation of FunctionsProc. No. 10053, par. (8), , 85 F.R. 39826, authorized The Secretary of Commerce to exercise the authority of the President under subsec. (a) of this section to establish or designate an office within the Department of Commerce to carry out the functions set forth in that subsection.June 29, 2020

children.heading: United States Section of Secretariat
children.label: a United States Section of Secretariat

children.heading: Establishment or designation of office
children.label: 1 Establishment or designation of office
children.text: The President is authorized to establish or designate within the Department of Commerce an office to serve as the United States Section of the Secretariat established under article 30.6 of the USMCA.

children.label: A
children.text: carry out its functions within the Secretariat to facilitate the operation of the USMCA, including the operation of section D of chapter 10 and chapter 31 of the USMCA; and

children.label: i
children.text: panels established under chapter 31 of the USMCA, including under Annex 31–A (relating to the Facility-Specific Rapid Response Labor Mechanism);

children.label: ii
children.text: technical advisers and experts provided for under chapter 31 of the USMCA;

children.label: iii
children.text: binational panels and extraordinary challenge committees established under section D of chapter 10 of the USMCA; and

children.label: iv
children.text: binational panels and extraordinary challenge committees established under NAFTA for matters covered by article 34.1 of the USMCA (relating to transition from NAFTA).

children.heading: Treatment of office under Freedom of Information Act
children.label: 3 Treatment of office under Freedom of Information Act
children.text: The office established or designated under paragraph (1) shall not be considered an agency for purposes of .section 552 of title 5
the operations of the office established or designated under subsection (a)(1); and

children.label: A
children.text: panels established under chapter 31 of the USMCA, including under Annex 31–A (relating to the Facility-Specific Rapid Response Labor Mechanism);

children.label: B
children.text: binational panels and extraordinary challenge committees established under section D of chapter 10 of the USMCA; and

children.label: C
children.text: binational panels and extraordinary challenge committees established under NAFTA for matters covered by article 34.1 of the USMCA (relating to transition from NAFTA).

children.heading: Reimbursement of certain expenses
children.label: c Reimbursement of certain expenses
children.text: If the Canadian Section or the Mexican Section of the Secretariat provides funds to the United States Section during any fiscal year as reimbursement for expenses in connection with dispute settlement proceedings under section D of chapter 10 or chapter 31 of the USMCA, or under chapter 19 of NAFTA, the United States Section may, notwithstanding , retain and use such funds to carry out the functions described in subsection (a)(2).section 3302 of title 31
